summary: Comprehensive backend (pytest, 14 tests) + frontend (Playwright) testing for Zaraj Gold Persian jewelry e-commerce. 13/14 backend tests pass. Frontend RTL, shop, auth, admin panel, cart all functional. Found 1 critical backend bug: GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 because Stripe can't find session immediately after creation.

critical.issue: Returns 500 InternalServerError. emergentintegrations.stripe raises CheckoutError: 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...' when polling immediately after POST /api/checkout/session created it. This breaks /checkout/success polling flow (CheckoutSuccess.jsx). Needs try/except around stripe.get_checkout_status to gracefully return last-known DB status, or a retry.

integration_issues.issue: Because /api/checkout/status returns 500, CheckoutSuccess.jsx polling will fail and user will not see payment confirmation.

action_items: Fix GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id}: wrap stripe.get_checkout_status in try/except; on failure return DB-stored payment_status instead of 500. Also consider a small delay or eventual-consistency retry.
Optional: seed_admin is non-idempotent (doesn't update password if admin exists with different pwd) - per playbook guidance. Currently not blocking since admin/admin123 works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 358: stripe.get_checkout_status exception not caught -> 500 to frontend. Should catch CheckoutError and fall back to db.payment_transactions stored status.
Admin seed on line 444-454 only inserts if missing; won't re-hash/update password on env change (playbook expects idempotent update).
CORS: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec (browsers reject). Use explicit origins list from CORS_ORIGINS env.
Cookie secure=False - acceptable for dev but should be True in prod HTTPS.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@zarajgold.ir / admin123 (verified login + admin role). Test users registered on-the-fly with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: 10 products + 1 admin auto-seeded on startup. Verified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py run via pytest. Only failing test is test_checkout_creates_session due to real Stripe API error on immediate status poll. Frontend routes verified: /, /shop, /login, /cart, /admin, /admin/products, /product/{id}. All data-testids present and working: login-email, login-password, login-submit, add-to-cart-btn, checkout-btn, new-product-btn.
rca of the issue: POST /api/checkout/session successfully creates a Stripe session and returns cs_test_ id. Immediately calling GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} causes emergentintegrations to call Stripe API which returns InvalidRequestError 'No such checkout.session'. Likely the session id returned by create is not yet queryable or there's an API key mismatch between create/retrieve paths in emergentintegrations. Backend does not handle the CheckoutError exception and propagates as 500. Fix: wrap call in try/except CheckoutError, return {status:'open', payment_status:'initiated'} from DB until webhook updates it.
